title: The Deific and Sometimes Creepy Side of Rei Lilith
display_title: The Deific and Sometimes Creepy Side of Rei Lilith
wikitext:
[]: The Deific and Sometimes Creepy Side of Rei/Lilith             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Mar 04, 2014 5:06 pm Bagheera wrote: The fact she has that power and didn't give any thought to how to use it beyond deciding who would make her choices for her is both frightening and contemptible. I find it astonishing even now that Asuka gets so much flak for being a bratty teenager while Rei is effectively ignored even though she was the willing instrument of the greatest act of genocide in the history of mankind.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Mar 04, 2014 5:29 pm NemZ wrote: Oh come now Bags... If you're going to insist everywhere else that people are going to come back from 3I it isn't fair for you to toss around the term Genocide. They aren't dead unless they stay dead.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Tue Mar 04, 2014 7:51 pm As presented, she's just . . . kinda lame, really. And Japan, so sasuga, but still.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Mar 05, 2014 3:14 am [s]In a perfect world I'd probably split it, yes, but the replies have gotten rather intertwined so that a clean break seems impossible. Maybe the 'thread surgeon' would like a crack at it?[/s] [Got it. - Monk] Rei IV wrote: Rei/Lilith, in a sense, is in a position to have a less than charitable view on her creation/children, yes, including Shinji, especially with humanity's mean streak against her.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Wed Mar 05, 2014 8:04 am Eh... I'd think her view of humans in general isn't so much dislike as just disinterest (a sentiment I beleive is shown in her poelm's line about flowers all the same, all without purpose) and not really caring about their welfare. I'd like to think Shinji is an exception (though she probably did dislike him at first for taking eva 01 and Gendo's favor from her), and perhaps Misato. She likely actively dislikes Asuka, Ritsuko and Gendo (in the end). Everyone else is just part of the scenery.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 05, 2014 1:48 pm NemZ wrote: Eh... I'd think her view of humans in general isn't so much dislike as just disinterest (a sentiment I beleive is shown in her poelm's line about flowers all the same, all without purpose) and not really caring about their welfare. I'd like to think Shinji is an exception (though she probably did dislike him at first for taking eva 01 and Gendo's favor from her), and perhaps Misato. She likely actively dislikes Asuka, Ritsuko and Gendo (in the end). Everyone else is just part of the scenery.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Wed Mar 05, 2014 2:14 pm But that wouldn't be the first time of Eva saying one and doing/showing opposite.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Wed Mar 05, 2014 2:58 pm Bagheera wrote: But doesn't that fly in the face of what she tells Shinji right before Operation Yashima? She seems to think she has a bond with all of humanity, that seems like more than disinterest to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 05, 2014 3:11 pm Rei IV wrote: Yeah but I'm pretty sure when coming to terms with her actual, alien, space-deity/god nature, which we can assume she's tried to suppress/downplay/deny/ignore, makes that all kind of moot. That and being violated by Armisael, coming back from the dead, being used as a prop frequently...I think she's in position to rethink about humanity, her so-called children. I mean, would it be that stretch to think that Rei could have contempt and disdain for others? She's generally disinterested and apathetic. Why else would she fuck with Shinji's psyche if she allegedly likes/cares for the kid?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Mar 05, 2014 3:36 pm It might be a tranalsation thing, but what she actually said is that she has a bond to all people through piloting. Bonds, however, can be either a positive connection or a snare holding you down. I'm suggesting she meant the latter while Shinji read it as the former.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Mar 05, 2014 5:05 pm It might also be that since due to her alien and extremely ancient nature, her concept of "wellbeing" for her children is broader : as long as the souls of mankind are safe and happy, she's content. After all she's a FAR soul put on a giant marshmallow body, then on a cloned Lilin body three times, then back into the giant marshmallow then fused with her "antithesis", ADAM, so maybe changing shape isn't something that shocks her anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Mar 05, 2014 5:16 pm That is one thing worth bearing in mind. Rei has, as Lilith, been suffering for millions of years. I think it's safe to say she's not going to be entirely right in the head after multiple bodyswaps, millennia of agony, and having her soul casually vivisected and placed inside a puny, rotting body.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 05, 2014 5:38 pm Squigsquasher wrote: That is one thing worth bearing in mind. Rei has, as Lilith, been suffering for millions of years. I think it's safe to say she's not going to be entirely right in the head after multiple bodyswaps, millennia of agony, and having her soul casually vivisected and placed inside a puny, rotting body.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Mar 05, 2014 5:43 pm Wasn't she unearthed with the Lance of Longinus impaled in her? I can hardly imagine being trapped underground with a sentient spike of metal embedded into you would allow for a restful sleep. Just because she's dormant doesn't mean she's not in pain- heck, people can be comatose and be aware of what's going on around them. Also, she will have regained all her memories of being Lilith in 3rd impact, or so I would have thought.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 05, 2014 5:50 pm Squigsquasher wrote: Wasn't she unearthed with the Lance of Longinus impaled in her?

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Wed Mar 05, 2014 5:52 pm Hmm, my memory is rusty. I need to rewatch the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Wed Mar 05, 2014 6:19 pm She was still crucified, and why she was or by whom never were explained. She left her cross quite easily, but that was only after becoming the Kaworu x Rei god. The cross always has been the symbol of torturous death. It's possible that Nerv put her on it, but what, was there a fully-ensouled Lilith walking around in Terminal Dogma before they bound her and made Shogoki from her flesh and tore out her soul? Since we have no evidence* of any intervening civilization, if Lilin didn't do it the first Ancestors must have, and in that case it's probably a set-up to allow her to create life. She is perpetually sacrificed to bring Lilin into existence. *(Some of the proposals I saw on the wiki said things about a Second Ancestral race, but since the Classified Information doesn't mention them I'm going to guess they were removed from the backstory.)

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 05, 2014 6:47 pm Lavinius wrote: She was still crucified, and why she was or by whom never were explained.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Wed Mar 05, 2014 6:58 pm Bagheera wrote: We have no reason to think she was in that position before they got their hands on her.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Mar 05, 2014 7:01 pm Lavinius wrote: That works both ways, you know. We have no reason to think she was.
summary: []: The Deific and Sometimes Creepy Side of Rei/Lilith             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Mar 04, 2014 5:06 pm Bagheera wrote: The fact she has that power and didn't give any thought to how to use it beyond deciding who would make her choices for her is both frightening and contemptible. I find it astonishing even now that Asuka gets so much flak for being a bratty teenager while Rei is effectively ignored even though she was the will
